CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2016 Continued To Dec-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Dancing Pines Distillery, LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted with twenty days leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for bad faith breach of contract and fraud in the inducement and denied as to the fourth through seventh causes of action for various common counts and specific performance. The two tort claims are pled in conclusory language and do not allege ultimate facts to support those claims. Plaintiff is given leave to correct these deficiencies if it can do so in good faith. The common counts claims are adequately pled and are permissible and routine claims alleged as alternatives to a breach of contract claim under the facts alleged. Specific performance is technically not a claim, but a remedy. However, because plaintiff has alleged the requisites for such a remedy, no useful purpose is achieved by granting the motion as to the seventh cause of action and then having plaintiff allege specific performance as an alternative remedy under its first cause of action. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576 =302/hk
